exemplar.title:
To what extent has Apple maintained its financial leadership in the global tech industry through the combination of Just-in-time production and market
positioning strategies?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has Apple maintained its financial leadership in the global tech industry through the combination of Just-in-time production and market
positioning strategies?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Introduction provides clear historical and contextual framing of Appleâs financial leadership.
Research question is clearly stated and effectively connected to the investigation.
Methodology draws on an appropriate mix of quantitative and qualitative sources.
weaknesses: The justification for the 2021â24 timeframe is only justified by data availability, lacking analytical depth.
Research question remains somewhat broad, combining two major strategic areas under one inquiry.
Frameworks (SWOT, Porterâs Five Forces, etc.) are selected without explicit rationale for their relative appropriateness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates accurate and effective communication of topic, a wellâfocused research question, and an appropriate range of methods. Minor gaps in analytic justification of period choice and framework selection prevent a perfect mark, placing the criterion at 5 of 6.

strengths: Source material is applied in a mostly relevant and appropriate manner to support arguments.
Business management concepts (JIT, lean, marketing frameworks) are clearly explained and coherently integrated.
Terminology (ROA, ROE, Porterâs Five Forces, 7Ps) is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Some source applications remain descriptive rather than analytically synthesized.
Occasional reliance on nonârigorous secondary compilations (e.g., blogs) detracts from academic rigor.
Raw financial data are sometimes presented without immediate interpretation, limiting their impact.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate demonstrates clear, coherent subject knowledge and generally appropriate application of sources and terminology. Minor lapses in analytical depth and source rigour constrain this to 4 of 6.

strengths: Research draws on a solid secondaryâdata base that is consistently relevant to the research question.
Arguments link JIT production and market positioning to financial performance with clear causal claims.
Some critical awareness of external risks (supplyâchain disruptions, geopolitical factors) enriches the evaluation.
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally asserts causality without deeply examining alternative explanations or counterâevidence.
Discussion and evaluation lack systematic weighting of the relative impacts of operations versus marketing.
Conclusions restate findings but do not offer actionable recommendations or further research directions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation shows good research relevance and clear analytical links between strategy and financial outcomes, but analysis and evaluation remain partially developed. The work therefore sits in the lower half of the 7â9 band, meriting 7 of 12.

strengths: The overall structure (title page, contents, introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography) supports reader navigation.
Layout conventions (consistent font, page numbering, labeled figures/tables) are largely observed.
weaknesses: Duplicate section numbering and occasional oversized images disrupt structural clarity.
Citation styles and worksâcited formatting show inconsistencies that affect professional polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets core structural and formatting requirements but includes enough lapses in numbering and citation consistency to limit the score to 3 of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**The research question is clearly stated and recognisable (âTo what extent has Apple maintained its financial leadership â¦ ?â).** It signals the key variablesâfinancial leadership, JIT production and market positioningâand implicitly sets temporal boundaries.  
â¢ **Partial focus**: Combining two substantial strategic areas (operations and marketing) under one âto what extentâ inquiry risks breadth over depth; causal pathways are not sharply delimited, and the dependent variable (âfinancial leadershipâ) is defined mainly through profitability ratios, leaving scope and measurement somewhat open-ended.  
â¢ **Connection to discussion is mostly maintained** through ratio analysis and marketing frameworks, yet occasional digressions (e.g., generic brand-value tables) reveal only moderate precision. These characteristics align with the 3-4 band; the clearer statement justifies the upper mark of 4.

**A coherent argument links JIT production and marketing to financial performance, culminating in a conclusion that answers the RQ.** The student references risks (e.g., supply-chain disruptions, debt leverage) and acknowledges data limitations, showing some evaluative thinking.  
â¢ **Critical evaluation is present but partial.** Alternative explanations (e.g., innovation pipeline, macroeconomic forces) are mentioned only briefly, and the weighting of JIT versus marketing in driving leadership is not systematically assessed.  
â¢ The discussion is therefore âwell-developed with partially critical evaluationâ, consistent with the 4-6 band; limited synthesis and relatively generic recommendations place it at score 4.

**The student demonstrates clear understanding of Business Management concepts**âJIT, lean production, marketing mix, Porterâs Five Forces, and financial ratios are correctly defined and generally well-explained.  
â¢ **Coherence and integration**: Concepts are woven into the narrative to interpret Appleâs performance, showing a grasp of how operations and marketing interact with financial outcomes.  
â¢ **Limitations remain**: Explanations can be formulaic, occasionally restating textbook points without critical nuance (e.g., unqualified claims about high debt automatically boosting ROE). The knowledge base is thus clear but not consistently deep, corresponding to the upper end of the 3-4 band (score 4).

**Terminology such as ROA, ROE, JIT, lean, 7Ps, Porterâs Five Forces and âpremium pricingâ is used accurately and regularly.** The essay correctly computes and interprets financial ratios, applies strategic frameworks and employs subject-specific language fluently.  
â¢ **Minor lapses** appearâe.g., occasional grammatical slips and an imprecise use of âfinancial leadershipâ, but these do not impede comprehension.  
â¢ Consistency is good, yet not flawless; citations sometimes lack detail and certain terms (e.g., âluxurious brandâ) are more generic marketing language. Overall, the strand aligns with the criteria for the 3-4 band at a strong level, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

**Analysis moves beyond description through ratio interpretation, SWOT, BCG and Porter applications,** linking JIT efficiencies to financial metrics and market outcomes. Calculations are correct and used to support claims about profitability and asset utilisation.  
â¢ **However, depth is variable.** Comparisons with competitors are brief, causality is often asserted rather than critically examined, and some frameworks (e.g., 7Ps) are applied at a surface level without weighing counter-evidence or limitations.  
â¢ Conclusions are generally supported but not rigorously challenged; thus the work fits the lower end of the 4-6 band, earning a score of 4 for offering clear yet moderately developed analysis.

**Source material is largely relevant and integrated into the narrative.** Financial statements, industry reports and academic studies are quoted to substantiate JIT benefits and marketing outcomes.  
â¢ **Application is mostly appropriate**âratios are calculated correctly and linked to arguments, and literature on lean/JIT and positioning is cited when analysing Appleâs strategies. Nevertheless, some citations are descriptive (e.g., lists of market-share statistics) without deeper linkage to the research question, and in a few places sources such as blogs or secondary compilations (Backlinko, RefurbMe) lack academic rigour.  
â¢ The balance between data presentation and analytical application is adequate but not exemplary; this meets the expectations of the 3-4 band, meriting a score of 4.

**Range and relevance of sources/methods are generally appropriate.** The essay draws on Appleâs annual reports, Statista data, academic journals on JIT/marketing, and recognised business press, and it applies ratios, SWOT, Porter, BCG and 7Ps.  
â¢ **Evidence of informed selection is visible but not always explicit.** The student mentions using triangulation and cross-checking news articles, yet offers limited critical justification for why particular frameworks or data sets were chosen over others, and primary research is absent.  
â¢ The set of methods is serviceable and mostly relevant, supporting a competent investigation; however, the lack of primary data and limited discussion of source limitations place the work at the top of the 3-4 band, earning a score of 4.

**The research base is solid and largely relevant to the question.** Financial statements, industry databases, and peer-reviewed studies on JIT and positioning provide sufficient evidence to address Appleâs performance between 2021-24.  
â¢ **Coverage is good but not exhaustive.** The investigation relies almost entirely on secondary data; no primary research or internal interviews are attempted. Nevertheless, multiple perspectives (financial, operational, strategic) are incorporated, and most data points link back to the RQ.  
â¢ The breadth and pertinence place the work in the 4-6 band; some uneven scrutiny of source credibility and limited primary data restrain it from the top of the band, warranting a mid-band score of 5.

**Basic layout conventions are respected**: consistent font, page numbering, table of contents with page references, appropriately labelled figures/tables, and an alphabetised works-cited list.  
â¢ **Formatting lapses** include occasional inconsistent citation styles, placeholder links (e.g., â[Show source](#)â), and some oversized images that disrupt pagination. These issues suggest partial, not full, mastery of formatting guidelines.  
â¢ Given that most, but not all, elements are correctly applied, the work meets but does not exceed expectations, positioning it at score 3 within the 3-4 band.

**Clarity and completeness of the topic explanation are strong.** The introduction identifies Appleâs financial leadership, specifies the twin lenses of JIT production and market positioning, and delineates a clear 2021-24 time frame. This framing situates the investigation convincingly within Business Management, signalling relevance for multiple stakeholders.  
â¢ **Depth of contextualization is good but not flawless.** The student outlines Appleâs evolution, competitive context and strategic importance, yet the rationale for choosing exactly 2021-24 is only briefly justified (âaccess to useful and reliable dataâ) rather than analytically defended.  
â¢ **Overall, communication is accurate and effective**, with only minor gaps in precision and depth; hence the strand fits comfortably in the upper band, but some lack of nuance and critical framing suggests a score of 5 rather than 6.

**The essay follows the expected EE structure**: title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, methodology, analysis sections, conclusion, and works cited, which supports reader navigation.  
â¢ **Minor structural issues** emergeâduplicate section numbering (two Section IVs), occasional imbalance in sub-section length, and some data tables that break narrative flow without clear signposting.  
â¢ While broadly appropriate and meeting core requirements, the imperfections indicate the lower half of the 3-4 band; hence a score of 3 is awarded.

annotations.comment: Quick Ratio excludes inventory but the calculation is not explicitly explained; clarify which current assets are included or omitted.
Comparing Appleâs liquidity ratios with industry examples like Microsoft adds valuable context to the financial analysis.
The debt-to-equity ratio interpretation could be deepened by discussing interest coverage or debt maturity structure to assess leverage risk.
Claims about Samsungâs margin ranges are made without citation; provide sources to substantiate comparative analysis.
The word count is provided at the start of the document, demonstrating compliance with IB formatting guidelines.
The inclusion of a clear, descriptive title appropriately labels the investigation and meets IB formatting requirements.
Frameworks and tools are listed without clear justification for their selection; explain why SWOT, Porterâs Five Forces, and other methods are most appropriate.
The research question is clearly stated but combines two major strategic areas, risking an overly broad scope; consider narrowing focus to strengthen analytical depth.
The table of contents is comprehensive and wellâorganized, facilitating easy navigation through the investigation.
Two separate sections are both labeled âIV,â creating confusion; renumber subsections to maintain a coherent structure.
The introduction provides a clear historical overview of Appleâs evolution, effectively contextualizing the investigation.
The choice of the 2021â24 timeframe is justified only by data availability; include analytical reasons for this periodâs strategic relevance.
The methodology outlines a balanced mix of qualitative and quantitative sources, indicating thoughtful research planning.
The literature review demonstrates effective use of diverse sources to define JIT and positioning concepts precisely.
The literature review summaries are descriptive; deepen analysis by synthesizing how each study directly informs the research question.
Figure 2 presents the condensed balance sheets clearly with appropriate labels, facilitating rapid comprehension of asset and liability trends.
Presenting raw financial data without accompanying interpretation limits analytical impact; integrate key observations alongside figures.
The cash flow statement is formatted consistently with previous figures, maintaining a professional presentation.
The gross profit margin formula lacks explicit unit labels; state whether figures are in billions to avoid reader confusion.
Calculating Appleâs gross profit margin and comparing it to competitors demonstrates sound quantitative application of financial ratios.
Sequential presentation of multiple ratio calculations (NPM, ROA, ROE, Current Ratio) clearly demonstrates analytical rigor.
Lean production principles are described without critical reflection on potential trade-offs, such as increased supplier complexity or quality control challenges.
Linking supply chain partnerships and geopolitical risks demonstrates critical awareness of external factors impacting financial stability.
The JIT discussion reiterates stock turnover figures; reference earlier analysis to avoid redundancy and maintain reader engagement.
Integrating Appleâs sustainability goals with JIT efficiency demonstrates sophisticated linkage of operational strategy and environmental impact.
Examples of kaizen and automated quality control illustrate effective practical application of lean principles in Appleâs operations.
The âProcessâ element of the 7Ps is incomplete; ensure all marketing mix components are fully developed for a coherent analysis.
The 7Ps analysis of product, price, place, promotion, and people demonstrates strong command of marketing concepts.
Figure 5 lists zero values for 2024 revenue data, which appears incorrect; verify data entries or annotate placeholders clearly.
Identifying shifts in Mac market share and linking them to M-series CPU performance strengthens the analysis of vertical integration.
The conclusion restates findings but offers no specific recommendations or suggestions for further research; include actionable next steps.
Discussing Appleâs 17% global smartphone market share adds valuable productâlevel insight to the strategic marketing analysis.
The horizontal bar chart and its accompanying table duplicate content; streamline presentation to avoid redundancy.
Porterâs Five Forces descriptions lack quantified assessments; include ratings or scales to indicate the relative strength of each force.
The concise summary of competitive pressures demonstrates proficient application of the Porterâs Five Forces framework.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes how JIT production and market positioning jointly underpin Appleâs financial leadership.
The works cited list demonstrates breadth but contains inconsistent formatting; apply a uniform citation style throughout.
The extensive range of sources reflects diligent secondary research and supports a comprehensive investigation.
